macro.title: Growing micro markets were a single-family outlier in late 2025
macro.what: NAHB’s Home Building Geography Index shows single-family construction weakening across every county type in late 2025 except micro counties, up 1.6%
macro.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
macro.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

macro.title: The 94% problem: What real estate agents aren’t being told about MLS success rates
macro.what: The 94% sold on MLS talking point uses only closed sales, excluding listings that never sold, which changes the real success rate.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Market Jitters Drive Mortgage Rates Up, Sending Some Would-Be Homebuyers to the Sidelines
macro.what: Higher housing costs, along with economic uncertainty that comes with the Iran war, are causing some house hunters to think twice
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Cleveland Remains a Beacon of Affordability for Homebuyers
housing.what: As many Americans consider buying a home this spring, they may want to take a closer look at the Cleveland metro area
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Case-Shiller index shows home price growth cooled further in January
housing.what: Case-Shiller shows January home prices up 0.9% year over year, 0.2% month over month, with Tampa down 2.5% and NYC up 4.9%.
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.what: In dollar terms, there’s $347 billion worth of stale listings in the U.S., more than ever before for this time of year

notables.title: Homeowner Assistance Fund backstopped vulnerable borrowers, study finds
notables.what: RIHA reviews the $10B HAF program, showing 90% of funds went to borrowers below AMI and many had nontraditional financing.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

notables.title: DC-area agent launches hyperlocal site to explain post-NAR commission changes
notables.what: Real estate agent launches an AI-built, hyperlocal platform to explain post-NAR commission rules, costs and strategy to buyers and sellers.

notables.title: Study: Most real estate deals require multiple curative steps before title insurance
notables.what: ALTA’s new study shows most real estate deals require extensive title research, curative work and fraud prevention before closing.
notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

notables.title: The ‘end-to-end’ title tech system has given way to something better

notables.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
